description: Miatta Fahnbulleh was Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State at the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government from 6 September 2025 to 12 May 2026. She was previously Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State at the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero between 9 July 2024 and 6 September 2025. She was elected as the MP for Peckham in July 2024. Education Miatta studied philosophy, politics and economics at the University of Oxford and obtained a PhD degree in economic development from the London School of Economics. Career outside politics Miatta was the Chief Executive of the New Economics Foundation from 2017 to 2023 and previously worked at the IPPR as the Director of Policy and Research. She was the Head of Cities in the policy unit at the Cabinet Office from 2011 to 2013.

She was previously Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State at the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero between 9 July 2024 and 6 September 2025.

She was elected as the MP for Peckham in July 2024.

Miatta was the Chief Executive of the New Economics Foundation from 2017 to 2023 and previously worked at the IPPR as the Director of Policy and Research. She was the Head of Cities in the policy unit at the Cabinet Office from 2011 to 2013.

She was previously Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State at the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero between 9 July 2024 and 6 September 2025.

• English devolution 
  • Mayoral Combined Authorities and pan-regional bodies

details.body:
Miatta Fahnbulleh was Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State at the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government from 6 September 2025 to 12 May 2026. She was previously Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State at the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero between 9 July 2024 and 6 September 2025.

She was elected as the MP for Peckham in July 2024.

Education

Miatta studied philosophy, politics and economics at the University of Oxford and obtained a PhD degree in economic development from the London School of Economics.

Career outside politics

Miatta was the Chief Executive of the New Economics Foundation from 2017 to 2023 and previously worked at the IPPR as the Director of Policy and Research. She was the Head of Cities in the policy unit at the Cabinet Office from 2011 to 2013.

The minister is responsible for:

  • Warm Homes Plan
  • fuel poverty
  • energy consumer issues, including; domestic energy bills, Ofgem consumer elements, the energy price cap and smart meters
  • domestic energy efficiency
  • public sector decarbonisation
  • clean heat, including the Boiler Upgrade Scheme
  • heat networks
  • business and commercial buildings decarbonisation
  • Ofgem Review
